address: 99/9 Moo 4, Kho Hong, Hat Yai District

description.en:
## Overview

Central Festival Hat Yai is the largest shopping mall in southern Thailand, a gleaming modern complex that opened in December 2013 and spans an impressive 250,000 square meters with over 250 retail shops. Located on Kho Hong Road about 3 kilometers from Hat Yai city center, the mall has become the commercial heart of the region, drawing shoppers from across southern Thailand and neighboring Malaysia. For travelers, it offers air-conditioned relief from the heat, international dining options, and all the conveniences of a world-class shopping center.

## Shopping

The mall houses a carefully curated mix of international and Thai brands spread across multiple floors. Major international retailers include Uniqlo, H&M, and Zara, alongside Thai fashion brands and local boutiques. The ground floor features a mix of cosmetics, accessories, and lifestyle brands, while upper floors are dedicated to fashion, electronics, and home goods. The Strawberry Club on the third floor is a local favorite for incredibly affordable souvenirs, trinkets, and small gifts. Prices at international brands are often slightly lower than in Bangkok, and the VAT refund counter makes the mall even more attractive for international visitors spending over 2,000 THB.

## Dining and Entertainment

The upper floors feature an expansive food court serving both Thai and international cuisine at affordable prices, with most dishes between 50 and 120 THB. Sit-down restaurants include Japanese spots like Honjiro Sushi, Korean BBQ, Italian, and Thai fine dining options. The entertainment wing houses a modern cinema complex showing both Thai and international films, plus a bowling alley and arcade zone. The basement level contains a large Tops supermarket where you can stock up on Thai snacks, spices, and products to take home.

## Practical Information

Central Festival Hat Yai is open daily from 10:30 AM to 9:00 PM with free admission. The mall is about 10 minutes by songthaew or taxi from Hat Yai Railway Station. Free shuttle buses operate from several points in the city center. The mall has ample parking for those driving. Air conditioning throughout makes it an ideal escape during the hottest hours of the day, and free Wi-Fi is available on all floors.
description.nl: Modern winkelcentrum met internationale merken restaurants en entertainment

best_time_to_visit: Midday to escape the heat; weekdays for fewer crowds
tips: Visit the Strawberry Club on the third floor for extremely affordable souvenirs and small gifts
The basement Tops supermarket is the best place to buy Thai snacks and spices to take home
Free shuttle buses run from several points in the city center to the mall
